title: Type validation finding kinds and severities
description:
Type the validation finding kinds and severities (report stringly findings). JSON output must stay byte-compatible.

## Sites
- `validation/graph.rs:98-102` (`is_config_error`) — detects the config-error kind via `message.starts_with(CONFIG_ERROR_PREFIX)`, which drives a real transition guard at `commands/mod.rs:692-708`. Add an `is_config_error: bool` (or a `FindingKind` enum) field to `GraphFinding`, set it in `config_error()`, and read the field instead of scanning the message.
- `validation/report.rs:39-49` — `ReportedFinding.severity` and `RuleOutcome.severity`/`scope` are `String` where `Severity`/`Scope` enums already exist; predicates round-trip via `.token()`. Derive `Serialize(rename_all="snake_case")` on both enums, type the fields, and reduce `is_error()`/`has_errors()` to `== Severity::Error`.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] The config-error transition guard reads a typed field (`is_config_error` bool or `FindingKind`) on `GraphFinding`; no `message.starts_with(CONFIG_ERROR_PREFIX)` classification remains in the control path.
- [ ] `ReportedFinding.severity` and `RuleOutcome.severity`/`scope` are typed `Severity`/`Scope`; `is_error()`/`has_errors()` are enum comparisons, not string token round-trips.
- [ ] Serialized validation JSON is byte-identical to before (snake_case), verified by an existing or added snapshot/round-trip test.
- [ ] `cargo-ci` and `code-review` pass.
